title: QLoRA / Training Engineering
notes: This studio engine is a planner, not a CUDA trainer.
bitsandbytes 4-bit and 8-bit training paths are CUDA-oriented and are not treated as native Apple MPS QLoRA.
Mac execution should use this planner to choose between CPU/MPS LoRA, remote CUDA QLoRA, or inference-focused local quantization stacks.

candidate_models.label: Small local instruct model
candidate_models.why: Good local LoRA/SFT teaching model; QLoRA is usually unnecessary at this scale.

candidate_models.label: 1B class open chat model
candidate_models.why: Still small enough for local planning; useful as a bridge between toy and 7B models.

candidate_models.label: 7B QLoRA target
candidate_models.why: Typical single-GPU QLoRA candidate on 24GB CUDA cards.

candidate_models.label: 13B memory pressure case
candidate_models.why: Shows where activation checkpointing, sequence length, and optimizer memory matter.

candidate_models.label: 70B distributed boundary
candidate_models.why: A planning example for multi-GPU sharding; not a local execution target.

profiles.name: mac_mps_lora_planning
profiles.description: Apple Silicon path: run fp16/bf16/fp32 LoRA or plan remote CUDA. Do not claim bitsandbytes QLoRA is native on MPS.

profiles.name: single_cuda_qlora_24gb
profiles.description: Common QLoRA workstation profile with bitsandbytes 4-bit base weights plus trainable LoRA adapters.

profiles.name: single_cuda_lora_24gb
profiles.description: Baseline fp16 LoRA profile, useful for comparing why QLoRA exists.

profiles.name: multi_cuda_deepspeed_zero3
profiles.description: DeepSpeed boundary example: approximate per-GPU memory with world_size-way parameter/gradient/optimizer sharding. Validate with real ZeRO/FSDP config.
